_about: HSK 3.0 band per Card, the audit trail behind cards.hsk_level. Each band was adjudicated by one Haiku agent anchored to a deterministic per-word lookup against seed/mandarin/hsk30-wordlist.json; a final consistency sweep flagged 3 outliers, all reviewed and approved by the owner (see correctedFrom/correction). Band 7 = the merged HSK 7-9 advanced band; 8 and 9 are never used. Applied to the deck by scripts/level-hsk.ts.

cards.rationale: All words are official list matches with maxBand 2 (英文); grammar is simple interrogative requiring no elevation.
All words are HSK 1-2 (maxBand 2 for 英文); grammar is simple yes/no interrogation with no advanced patterns.
All three words (你, 明白, 吗) are HSK 1 with no advanced grammar; simple yes-no question is HSK 1 level.
maxBand is 1 from the official HSK wordlist; simple statement with no advanced grammar.
All constituent words are HSK 1 (我, 不, 明白), and the simple subject-negation-verb structure contains no grammar complexity.
All words are HSK 1; '一点' is a compositional measure compound (一+点) explicitly listed in the rules as HSK 1 despite null lookup, and the grammar is simple S-V-O.
All matched words are HSK 1; unmatched 拼音 is compositional and practical, not advanced; grammar is simple imperative with no complex structures.
All words are HSK 1-3; 下来 (directional complement) determines maxBand 3; grammar is simple imperative with no complex structures.
maxBand is 3 from 指出; the unmatched word 范句 is compositional (example-phrase), and grammar is simple imperative with no complex structures.
Maxband 2 is justified by 意思; unmatched 这个 is compositional HSK-1 demonstrative and does not elevate the band.
Vocabulary maxBand is 3 from 念 (pronounce); 这个 is compositional; grammar is simple question syntax.
All matched vocabulary is HSK 1; 熊猫 (panda) is basic animal vocabulary, not advanced; grammar is a simple HSK-1 question.
All words present in official wordlist with maxBand 2 (遍); simple imperative grammar requires no elevation.
All matched words are HSK 1, the null word 一点 is a compositional elementary phrase, and the polite imperative grammar is straightforward with no complex constructions.
Maxband 3 from 下来 (xiàlái, resultative complement); grammar is standard HSK-3 polite-request structure without advanced constructions.
The conjunction 与 is HSK-6 vocabulary; though the phrase is conceptually simple, the vocabulary load determines the band.
The compound is absent from the wordlist; 基 (base) is HSK 5 and forms the hardest component; the specialized but compositional term is therefore HSK 5.
cards.correction: Sweep: aligned with its twin 序数 (ordinal numbers, HSK 4) — both metalinguistic header terms absent from the wordlist.

cards.rationale: 零 is a fundamental numeral unattested in HSK 3.0 but functionally equivalent to basic HSK-1 numerals and essential for beginner communication.
Fundamental HSK-1 numeral; the lookup is authoritative and matches maxBand directly.
二 is a basic HSK-1 number with no grammar complexity; maxBand lookup confirms band 1.
Basic numeral directly matched to HSK 1 in the official wordlist with no grammatical complexity.
四 is a basic HSK 1 number with no grammar complexity.
This is a basic numeral from HSK 1; the lookup is authoritative with no grammatical complexity.
Official HSK 1 lookup with maxBand 1; basic single-digit number with no complex grammar.
The character 七 (seven) is a basic number word appearing in HSK 1 vocabulary with no grammatical complexity; the lookup confirms band 1.
八 is HSK 1 basic vocabulary (the number 8), with no grammatical elevation needed.
The numeral 九 (9) is assigned band 1 in the official HSK 3.0 wordlist as a foundational basic numeral.
十 is listed as HSK 1 in the official wordlist; fundamental number/counting word with no grammar complexity.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十 + 一); rule explicitly assigns such compounds to HSK 1.
十二 is a compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十+二); absent from wordlist but clearly beginner-level vocabulary.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十 + 三), not advanced vocabulary.
十四 is a compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十 + 四), which per Rule 3 are HSK 1 regardless of wordlist absence.
十五 is a compositional number built from HSK-1 words (十 + 五) and does not require inflating to a higher band despite wordlist absence.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十 + 六).
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十 + 七); follows the explicit rule that numbers composed from HSK-1 elements are HSK 1.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 components (十 + 八); nulled entries for composite numbers are EASY, not hard.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (十+九), following the established pattern for basic numerals; null lookup should not inflate the band.
Compositional number from HSK-1 words 二 (two) + 十 (ten); applies Rule 3 exception for numbers built from basic parts.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (二+十+一); such basic numerals are HSK 1.
The number 二十二 is compositionally built from HSK-1 words (二, 十), making it HSK 1 despite the wordlist entry being null.
Compositional number formed from HSK-1 words 三 and 十; explicitly listed as HSK 1 in the guidelines.
This is a compositional number built from two HSK-1 words (四 four + 十 ten), making it HSK 1 despite missing from the official wordlist.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (五 + 十); standard pattern like 三十.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (六 + 十); numbers formed this way are HSK 1.
七十 is a compositional number made from HSK-1 words 七 and 十, hence HSK 1.
Compositional number from HSK-1 parts (八 + 十); numbers built from basic components are beginner-level regardless of wordlist coverage.
A compositional number (nine + ten) built entirely from HSK-1 parts, following the same pattern as 三十 and 二十二.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (一 + 百); follows the standard pattern for HSK-1 numerals.
Compositional number using basic HSK-1 parts (一, 百) and the fundamental zero (零); straightforward for any learner with basic counting vocabulary.
Compositional number built entirely from HSK-1 parts (一+百+零+三); rule 3 explicitly classifies such numbers as HSK 1.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 numerals; basic number-formation is level 1 vocabulary.
Compositional number built entirely from HSK-1 parts (一, 百, 二, 十); per Rule 3, such numbers are HSK 1 despite wordlist absence.
Compositional number built from HSK-1 parts (两 two + 百 hundred), matching the pattern of 十一, 三十, etc. standard in beginner phrasebooks.
Compositional number (一 + 千) built entirely from basic HSK-1 number parts; both components are fundamental to the Chinese counting system.
Compositional number (one × ten-thousand unit) built from HSK-1 parts; treat as HSK 1 per guidance.
Number built from HSK-1 parts (一+百+万); compositional, beginner-level vocabulary.
Compositional number (one + hundred-million unit); both components are basic number vocabulary, not advanced.
序 is not HSK 1-3 vocabulary; the formal term 序数 for ordinal numbers is pedagogically introduced after basic ordinals (第一, 第二, etc.) and belongs to HSK 4+.
Compositional ordinal from 第 (HSK 2 ordinal prefix) and 一 (HSK 1), making this common word HSK 2.
cards.correction: Sweep: aligned with its four siblings 第二/第三/第四/第五, all HSK 1 with the identical 第+numeral structure.

maxBand 2 from 可以; unmatched words are compositional (退换 = basic compound; 这个 = demonstrative+measure per rules); simple S-V-O grammar with question particle.

cards.correction: Sweep: its peers 手续费是多少？and 换外币的地方在哪儿？are both HSK 6 (same financial register, same frame), and 兑 is treated as HSK 7-9 elsewhere in this deck.
